leaf_node_count: How many leaf nodes are there?
Count the nodes with no children. How many are there?
What is the number of leaf nodes?
How many nodes have neither a left child nor a right child?
Find all childless nodes in the tree. How many are there?
internal_node_count: How many internal nodes are there?
Count the nodes that have at least one child. How many are there?
What is the number of non-leaf nodes?
How many nodes have a left child, a right child, or both?
Find all nodes with children in the tree. How many are there?
single_child_node_count: How many nodes have exactly one child?
Count the nodes with one child. How many are there?
What is the number of nodes that have only a left child or only a right child?
How many nodes have one child rather than zero or two?
Find every node with exactly one child. How many are there?
two_child_node_count: How many nodes have both a left child and a right child?
Count the nodes with two children. How many are there?
What is the number of nodes that have both children?
How many nodes branch to both a left and right child?
Find every node with two children. How many are there?
depth_level_node_count: How many nodes are at depth {target_depth}, where the root is depth 0?
Count the nodes at depth {target_depth}, counting the root level as depth 0.
What is the number of nodes on depth level {target_depth}?
Starting with the root at depth 0, how many nodes are at depth {target_depth}?
Find every node whose depth is {target_depth}. How many are there?
parent_label: What is the label of the parent of node {query_label}?
Which node is the parent of {query_label}?
Find node {query_label}'s parent. What label does that parent have?
What label is on the node directly above {query_label}?
Which label belongs to the parent node of {query_label}?
left_child_label: What is the label of the left child of node {query_label}?
Which node is the left child of {query_label}?
Find node {query_label}'s left child. What label does it have?
What label is on the child below-left of {query_label}?
Which label belongs to the left child of {query_label}?
right_child_label: What is the label of the right child of node {query_label}?
Which node is the right child of {query_label}?
Find node {query_label}'s right child. What label does it have?
What label is on the child below-right of {query_label}?
Which label belongs to the right child of {query_label}?
sibling_label: What is the label of the sibling of node {query_label}?
Which node shares the same parent as {query_label}?
Find node {query_label}'s sibling. What label does that sibling have?
What label is on the other child of {query_label}'s parent?
Which label belongs to the sibling node of {query_label}?
lowest_common_ancestor_label: What is the label of the lowest common ancestor of nodes {query_label_a} and {query_label_b}?
Find the lowest shared ancestor of {query_label_a} and {query_label_b}. What label does it have?
Which node is the lowest common ancestor of {query_label_a} and {query_label_b}?
For nodes {query_label_a} and {query_label_b}, what label is on their deepest shared ancestor?
Trace upward from {query_label_a} and {query_label_b}. What is the label of their lowest common ancestor?
preorder_kth_node_label: What is the label at position {traversal_position} in preorder traversal, counting from 1?
In preorder traversal, which node label is at position {traversal_position}?
Traverse root, then left subtree, then right subtree. What label is at position {traversal_position}?
Using preorder order, what label is visited at position {traversal_position}?
Counting preorder visits from 1, what label appears at position {traversal_position}?
inorder_kth_node_label: What is the label at position {traversal_position} in inorder traversal, counting from 1?
In inorder traversal, which node label is at position {traversal_position}?
Traverse left subtree, then root, then right subtree. What label is at position {traversal_position}?
Using inorder order, what label is visited at position {traversal_position}?
Counting inorder visits from 1, what label appears at position {traversal_position}?
postorder_kth_node_label: What is the label at position {traversal_position} in postorder traversal, counting from 1?
In postorder traversal, which node label is at position {traversal_position}?
Traverse left subtree, then right subtree, then root. What label is at position {traversal_position}?
Using postorder order, what label is visited at position {traversal_position}?
Counting postorder visits from 1, what label appears at position {traversal_position}?
level_order_kth_node_label: What is the label at position {traversal_position} in level-order traversal, counting from 1?
In level-order traversal, which node label is at position {traversal_position}?
Traverse the tree level by level from top to bottom and left to right. What label is at position {traversal_position}?
Using level-order order, what label is visited at position {traversal_position}?
Counting level-order visits from 1, what label appears at position {traversal_position}?
bst_search_terminal_label: Searching for key {target_key}, what is the label of the final visited node?
Follow the binary-search-tree search path for key {target_key}. Which node label is reached last?
If you search this BST for {target_key}, what label is on the terminal node of the search path?
Using the BST ordering rule, search for {target_key}. What node label ends the search?
What is the final node label on the search path for key {target_key}?
bst_insert_parent_label: If key {target_key} were inserted into this binary search tree, what existing node would be its parent?
Follow the BST insertion path for key {target_key}. Which existing node label would become the parent?
Where would key {target_key} attach? Give the label of the existing parent node.
Using the BST ordering rule, insert {target_key}. What label is on the parent node where it would attach?
What existing node label is the parent for a new key {target_key}?
heap_property_violation_label: Exactly one child has a smaller key than its parent. What is that child node's label?
Find the min-heap violation. Which child node label is smaller than its parent key?
Which child node violates the min-heap rule by being smaller than its parent?
The tree is intended to be a min-heap except for one parent-child violation. What is the violating child label?
What label is on the child node that breaks the min-heap parent-before-child order?

code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract: Use a valid JSON object with keys "annotation" and "answer" in that order for the final answer.
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract_answer_only: Use a valid JSON object with key "answer" for the final answer.
code_prompt_defaults.object_description: a labeled binary tree with the root at the top and left/right children shown by position
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_heap: a numeric-key binary tree intended to be a min-heap
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_count: set "answer" to the requested count as an integer
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_node_label: set "answer" to the answer node label as a string exactly as shown
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_numeric_key_label: set "answer" to the answer node key as a string exactly as shown
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_leaf_node_count: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points at the centers of every leaf node; use [] when the answer is zero
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_internal_node_count: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points at the centers of every internal node, meaning every non-leaf node; use [] when the answer is zero
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_single_child_node_count: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points at the centers of every node with exactly one child; use [] when the answer is zero
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_two_child_node_count: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points at the centers of every node with both left and right children; use [] when the answer is zero
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_depth_level_node_count: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points at the centers of every node at depth {target_depth}; use [] when the answer is zero
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_parent_label: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "child" and "parent"; each value is a [x,y] pixel point at that node center
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_left_child_label: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "parent" and "left_child"; each value is a [x,y] pixel point at that node center
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_right_child_label: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "parent" and "right_child"; each value is a [x,y] pixel point at that node center
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_sibling_label: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "node" and "sibling"; each value is a [x,y] pixel point at that node center
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_lowest_common_ancestor_l: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "node_a", "node_b", and "lowest_common_ancestor"; each value is a [x,y] pixel point at that node center
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_preorder_kth_node_label: set "annotation" to an ordered JSON array of [x,y] pixel points at node centers for the preorder traversal nodes from the first visited node through the answer node
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_inorder_kth_node_label: set "annotation" to an ordered JSON array of [x,y] pixel points at node centers for the inorder traversal nodes from the first visited node through the answer node
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_postorder_kth_node_label: set "annotation" to an ordered JSON array of [x,y] pixel points at node centers for the postorder traversal nodes from the first visited node through the answer node
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_level_order_kth_node_lab: set "annotation" to an ordered JSON array of [x,y] pixel points at node centers for the level-order traversal nodes from the first visited node through the answer node
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_bst_search_terminal_labe: set "annotation" to an ordered JSON array of [x,y] pixel points at node centers for the visited binary-search path from the root through the answer node
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_bst_insert_parent_label: set "annotation" to an ordered JSON array of [x,y] pixel points at node centers for the visited insertion path from the root through the parent node where the new key would attach
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_heap_property_violation_: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "parent" and "child"; each value is a [x,y] pixel point at that node center
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the minimal node-center point witnesses required by the question
